digest.en: Israel has begun intercepting Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla aid boats. The UAE's OPEC exit has been long in the works. Jaishankar's talks with Iran's Foreign Minister on the West Asia situation add another layer to the complex geopolitics in the region.
digest.hi: मैक्सिको में 10 अधिकारी ड्रग तस्करी में घिरे हैं। न्यूयॉर्क में उनके खिलाफ केस दर्ज किया गया है। ईरान के विदेश मंत्री ने लेबनान के स्पीकर से क्षेत्रीय हालात पर चर्चा की।

articles.title: Israel has begun intercepting Gaza-bound Global Sumud Flotilla aid boats
articles.summary: Israeli military speedboats have intercepted Gaza-bound aid boats from the Global Sumud Flotilla. Organisers say their vessels are being stopped. The flotilla aimed to deliver aid to Gaza. Organisers of a Gaza-bound aid mission say their vessels are being intercepted by Israeli military speedboats.

articles.title: UAE’s OPEC exit has been long in the works
articles.summary: The UAE's exit from OPEC has been planned for years. The Emirates have been dissatisfied with OPEC quotas. An academic says they chafed against the constraints. The Emirates have chafed against the constraints of OPEC quotas for years, says an academic.

articles.title: Israel intercepts Global Sumud boats, demands activists halt their mission
articles.summary: Israel has intercepted seven of 58 vessels from the Global Sumud Flotilla near Crete. The flotilla aimed to deliver aid to Gaza under blockade. Activists were demanded to halt their mission.

articles.title: Jaishankar holds talks with Iran's Foreign Minister on West Asia situation
articles.summary: Jaishankar held talks with Iran's Foreign Minister to discuss the West Asia situation and conflict. U. S. Congress will grill Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on the West Asia conflict on Wednesday.

articles.title: Powell to remain at Fed amid legal 'battering' by Trump administration
articles.summary: Jerome Powell will remain at the Fed after his term as chair ends on May 15. He will continue as a governor for a period to be determined. Powell made the announcement at a news conference.

articles.title: Trump Says US Has "A Shot" At Crewed Moon Landing Before Presidency Ends
articles.summary: Donald Trump says the US has a chance to land a crewed mission on the Moon before his presidency ends. He welcomed the Artemis II astronauts at the White House.

articles.title: Nuclear test ban treaty chief warns of rising global security threats as key states stall on
articles.summary: The head of the nuclear test ban treaty warned of rising global security threats due to key states stalling on ratification. This comes as tensions escalate globally. The treaty's effectiveness is hindered by major countries not signing on.

articles.title: Alleged Trump Assassin Took Selfie Moments Before Attack: Prosecutors
articles.summary: Prosecutors say the alleged Trump assassin took a selfie moments before the attack. The 31-year-old was then tackled by security guards. The incident is under investigation. The 31-year-old was tackled and detained in a chaotic scuffle with security guards.

articles.title: Key US senator to call for new efforts to prevent undersea cable sabotage
articles.summary: A key US senator will call for new efforts to prevent undersea cable sabotage. This move aims to protect critical infrastructure. The senator's push comes amid growing concerns over cable security.

articles.title: Arsenal cruelly denied penalty by VAR in Champions League draw at Atletico
articles.summary: Arsenal was denied a penalty by VAR in a Champions League semifinal draw against Atletico Madrid. The match ended 1-1. VAR's decision was controversial. Arsenal scored one penalty, but VAR controversially denied them another in a 1-1 semifinal draw at Atletico Madrid.

articles.title: New Florida map boosts Republican seats amid national redistricting fight
articles.summary: Florida's new map boosts Republican seats ahead of midterm elections. The state is among several redrawing electoral maps. The changes impact the national electoral landscape. Florida has become latest state to redraw map, as national electoral landscape transforms before midterm elections.

articles.title: Putin offered to help settle Iranian nuclear enrichment impasse
articles.summary: Vladimir Putin told Donald Trump he'd like to be involved in settling Iran's nuclear enrichment issue, according to Trump. The US has been trying to negotiate a deal with Iran. Details of Putin's offer are unclear.

articles.title: Why is the UAE quitting OPEC – and what’s the impact?
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates is quitting OPEC, citing the worst global energy crisis in modern times. The move is seen as a significant shift in the global energy landscape. The UAE's decision is effective January 2025.

articles.title: Ukraine asks Israel to seize ship carrying grain ‘stolen’ by Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine has asked Israel to seize a ship carrying grain allegedly stolen by Russia. Kyiv wants Israel to take grain samples and question crew members. The move is part of Ukraine's efforts to recover stolen assets.

articles.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran war and float temporary Ukraine ceasefire in call
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed a temporary Ukraine ceasefire and the Iran war during a recent phone call. Trump and Putin discuss Iran war and float temporary Ukraine ceasefire in call The Guardian.

articles.title: The Iran war now has a price tag ($25 billion), but still no end date
articles.summary: The US war in Iran has cost an estimated $25 billion over the past two months, according to the Pentagon, but a definitive end date remains unclear. The Pentagon estimates the war has cost $25 billion over the past two months.

articles.title: Iran’s rial currency hits record low as a shaky ceasefire with the US and Israel holds
articles.summary: Iran's rial currency has hit a record low as a shaky ceasefire with the US and Israel holds. The economic strain on Iran is evident in the currency's decline. Iran’s rial currency hits record low as shaky ceasefire with US and Israel holds AP News.

articles.title: Trump says US knows where Iran added military equipment during ceasefire
articles.summary: Donald Trump claims the US knows where Iran added military equipment during a ceasefire. The US has been monitoring Iran's military activities. Details of the equipment and its location are not publicly known.

articles.title: Trump and Putin hold phone call, discuss Iran’s nuclear program, ceasefire
articles.summary: Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin held a phone call to discuss Iran's nuclear program and ceasefire. The two leaders also reportedly discussed other issues, but details are scarce. The call is part of ongoing diplomatic efforts.

articles.title: US Has Spent $25 Billion So Far In War With Iran: Pentagon Official
articles.summary: The US has spent $25 billion so far in its war with Iran, according to a Pentagon official. The conflict began on February 28 with US strikes against Iranian targets.

articles.title: Trump, Putin Discuss Iran War And Ukraine In Over 90-Minute Call
articles.summary: Trump and Putin discussed Iran and Ukraine in a 90-minute call. The presidents "paid particular attention to the situation regarding Iran and in the Persian Gulf". Trump, Putin Discuss Iran War And Ukraine In Over 90-Minute Call.

articles.title: मैक्सिको के गवर्नर समेत 10 अधिकारी ड्रग तस्करी में घिरे, न्यूयॉर्क में दर्ज किया केस
articles.summary: मैक्सिको के गवर्नर समेत 10 अधिकारियों पर न्यूयॉर्क में ड्रग तस्करी का केस दर्ज किया गया है। इन पर अमेरिका में बड़ी मात्रा में ड्रग्स आयात करने का आरोप है। यह मामला अमेरिका की एक बड़ी कार्रवाई का हिस्सा है।

articles.title: ईरान को लेकर ट्रंप का बड़ा दावा- दुश्मनों के 82% हथियार तबाह, अब गोलियों से गिर रहे ड्रोन
articles.summary: अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति डोनाल्ड ट्रंप ने दावा किया कि ईरान के खिलाफ कार्रवाई में दुश्मनों के 82% हथियार तबाह हो गए हैं। अब ईरानी ड्रोन को गोलियों से गिराया जा रहा है। यह दावा अमेरिकी मीडिया में चर्चा में है।

articles.title: अगले माह भारत आएंगे रूसी विदेश मंत्री लावरोव: द्विपक्षीय संबंधों पर होगी चर्चा, ब्रिक्स बैठक में भी
articles.summary: रूसी विदेश मंत्री सर्गेई लावरोव अगले महीने भारत की यात्रा करेंगे। इस दौरान द्विपक्षीय संबंधों पर चर्चा होगी और ब्रिक्स बैठक में भी हिस्सा लेंगे। यह यात्रा भारत और रूस के बीच संबंधों को मजबूत करने के लिए महत्वपूर्ण होगी।

articles.summary: पाकिस्तान के प्रधानमंत्री शहबाज शरीफ ने अमेरिका-ईरान युद्ध के संभावित प्रभावों पर चिंता जताई है। उन्होंने कहा कि इससे देश की अर्थव्यवस्था को बड़ा झटका लग सकता है, खासकर तेल आयात बिल के मामले में।

articles.title: यूएन में भारत की दो टूक, नाविकों की सुरक्षा पर जताई चिंता
articles.summary: भारत ने संयुक्त राष्ट्र में आग्रह किया है कि होर्मुज जलमार्ग को जल्द से जल्द खोल दिया जाए। भारत ने नाविकों की सुरक्षा को लेकर भी चिंता जताई है। 'होर्मुज को जल्द खोलो': यूएन में भारत की दो टूक, नाविकों की सुरक्षा पर जताई चिंता Jagran।

articles.summary: ईरान की आक्रामक रणनीति के पीछे आर्थिक अवसर हो सकते हैं। ईरान के नेता युद्ध की स्थिति में भी अपने आर्थिक हितों को बढ़ावा देने की कोशिश कर रहे हैं। यह रणनीति उसके लिए फायदेमंद हो सकती है।
